meta.title: Simon Lab | Computational Engineering for Chemical Projects
meta.description: Simon Lab - Computational Engineering for Chemical Projects. Code-native workflows making projects traceable, verifiable, and AI-ready.

hero.title:
Computational Engineering
for Chemical Projects
hero.tagline: From lab bench to repository. Structuring engineering data as code—traceable, verifiable, and machine-readable from day one.

mission.title: The Mission
mission.p1: The chemical industry is falling behind. While software, finance, and manufacturing have embraced computational workflows, process engineering remains trapped in static documents—PDFs that die on submission, spreadsheets that drift with every revision, drawings that no system can parse.
mission.p2: This is the Broken Chain. Data created in one phase cannot flow to the next. Every handoff introduces error. Every project starts from scratch.
mission.p3: We are here to fix it.
mission.p4: The path forward is Project-as-Code: defining chemical projects in structured, version-controlled formats from the grant phase onward. When the project is code, the mathematics validate themselves. When the project is code, simulations compile from definitions. When the project is code, AI systems can read, check, and assist—not because we've automated engineering, but because we've made engineering computational.
mission.p5: This isn't about replacing engineers. It's about giving them infrastructure that keeps pace with the tools now available.

philosophy.title: The Project-as-Code Philosophy
philosophy.p1: Traditional chemical projects live in static documents. Grant proposals become PDFs. P&IDs become drawings. Simulations become spreadsheets. Each handoff introduces drift, each revision risks inconsistency, and ultimately—the project's logic dies in formats no system can parse.
philosophy.p3: We define project parameters in structured, version-controlled code from the grant phase forward. The mathematics are validated before the first funding request. The process flow compiles into a working simulation before the first line is drawn.
philosophy.p4: This isn't AI generating your plant. This is engineering intent captured with sufficient rigor that computational tools can read, validate, and optimize alongside your team.
philosophy.p5_cont: Projects that are Computational-Ready. Auditable at every revision. Traceable to every decision.

apps.title: Application Suite
apps.intro: The methodology is delivered through a purpose-built application suite—tools for code-native chemical engineering.

labelhash.name: LabelHash
labelhash.subtitle: Precision Labeling for Process Equipment
labelhash.description: ISO 20560-compliant label generation for pipelines, tanks, reactors, and vessels. Standardized identification with hazard symbols, color bands, and flow indicators. A practical utility for engineers.

processview.name: ProcessView
processview.subtitle: Visualizing the Flow
processview.description: Web-based P&ID visualization with embedded physics simulation. ProcessView compiles process definitions into working models—validate flow behavior and control logic before commissioning. Real-time SCADA interface for operational monitoring.

grantkernel.name: GrantKernel
grantkernel.subtitle: The Core Logic Engine
grantkernel.description: Converts research intent into computable project definitions. Define process parameters, material balances, and equipment specifications in structured code. Validates mathematics, enforces unit consistency, compiles compliant PDF outputs. The grant proposal becomes executable documentation.

labdaemon.name: LabDaemon
labdaemon.subtitle: Background Process for Experiment Integrity
labdaemon.description: Multi-agent assistant for laboratory workflows. Monitors experiment progress, prompts for data entry, ensures no measurement goes unrecorded. Persistent tracking from hypothesis to dataset.

plantmesh.name: PlantMesh
plantmesh.subtitle: 3D Environment from Code Definitions
plantmesh.description: Generates three-dimensional plant environments from ProcessView definitions. Spatial verification, clash detection, operator training—derived from the same source of truth.

capacityindex.name: CapacityIndex
capacityindex.subtitle: Business Intelligence from Simulation Data
capacityindex.description: Analytics from process definitions. Throughput projections, energy forecasts, capacity planning—no manual data extraction.

sitecommit.name: SiteCommit
sitecommit.subtitle: Version Control for Construction
sitecommit.description: Tracks specification changes, vendor substitutions, construction modifications with full revision history. Know what was specified versus what was built.

about.title: About Us
about.p1: Simon Lab delivers computational engineering services for the chemical industry. We combine process engineering expertise with code-native methodologies to structure projects that are auditable, traceable, and ready for the computational tools of today and tomorrow.
about.p2: Our work bridges the gap between traditional engineering practice and modern software infrastructure—ensuring that chemical projects can benefit from version control, validation, and computational assistance without sacrificing engineering rigor.

meta.title: LabelHash | Simon Lab
meta.description: LabelHash - ISO 20560-compliant pipeline label design toolkit by Simon Lab.

hero.tagline: Precision Labeling for Process Equipment — ISO 20560-compliant label generation for pipelines, tanks, reactors, and vessels.

features.title: Features
features.iso: ISO 20560 Compliance - Full compliance with international pipeline marking standards
features.medium: Medium Type Selection - Comprehensive database of chemical media with proper color coding
features.ghs: GHS Pictograms - Complete set of hazard pictograms for chemical safety labeling
features.hazards: Hazard Warnings - Automatic hazard band generation for dangerous substances
features.colors: Color Bands - Correct primary band colors based on medium category
features.flow: Flow Direction Arrows - Clear flow direction indicators for process clarity
features.preview: Live Preview - Real-time label preview with ruler overlays for accurate sizing
features.print: Print Ready - Export labels ready for professional printing

meta.title: ProcessView | Simon Lab
meta.description: ProcessView - Web-based SCADA system for chemical plant simulation and control by Simon Lab.

hero.tagline: Web-based P&ID visualization with embedded physics simulation for chemical plant design and control

features.title: Features
features.pid: Real-time P&ID Visualization - Interactive process and instrumentation diagrams with live updates
features.physics: Physics Simulation - Accurate simulation of process dynamics including flow, levels, and temperatures
features.control: Equipment Control - Control valves, pumps, and reactors with intuitive interface
features.annotation: Annotation Mode - Add notes, markups, and comments directly on the P&ID
features.pipeline: Pipeline Management - Track and manage all pipeline segments with detailed specifications
features.multitenant: Multi-tenant Architecture - Support for multiple isolated plant simulations
features.export: Print-ready Export - Generate technical drawing exports with proper title blocks
features.realtime: Real-time Updates - WebSocket-based communication for instant state synchronization

services.title: Our Services
services.p1: ProcessView services are ideally suited for back-of-envelope feasibility studies or as part of a basic engineering process design stage. Whether you're validating a concept or refining your process flow, we help you build a working simulation before any physical construction begins.
services.p2: This approach lets you test your future control room before the first weld is made. Validate operator workflows, check control logic, and identify potential issues—all in a simulated environment that mirrors your final installation.
